calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue
Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA COURTCALL): Atty. LONG X. DO, Esq. of Athene Law LLP (Tel: 415-722-5351) for the petitioner;
Attys. CAROLYN TSAI, Esq. and ANJANA GUNN, Esq. (Tel: 415-510-3539) appearing for the respondents.

HEARING RE: RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES, BRADLEY GILBERT's Motion To Change Venue.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

The motion by Respondents Bradley Gilbert and the California Department of Health Care Services to change venue to Kings County is granted. A case brought against a public agency or officer is properly brought "in the county in which the cause, or some part of the cause, arose." (Code Civ. Proc. 393(b).) The cause of action normally arises wherever the petitioner would be injured by the state action complained of, e.g., where the plaintiff resides or does business. "A cause arises in the county where the effects of the administrative decision are felt, not where the agency signs the challenged order or takes the challenged action." (Lipari v. Department of Motor Vehicles (1993) 16 Cal.App.4th 667, 670 fn. 2 [collecting authorities]; see Regents of University of California v. Superior Court (1970) 3 Cal.3d 529, 542 [venue against state board of regents for alleged improper expenditure of tax funds was in county where expenditures occurred].) It is irrelevant to the venue determination that petitioner hospital is affiliated with a multi-state health system that has accounts with a bank headquartered in San Francisco or that petitioner may be required to remit the overpaid moneys to respondent Department in Sacramento. As Respondents correctly observe, "if every party with a bank account in San Francisco could claim venue there, the venue statutes would be rendered meaningless." The petition challenges an administrative decision upholding respondents' audit of petitioner hospital's request for Medi-Cal reimbursements. (Pet. 18.) As Petitioner hospital is located in Hanford in Kings County, venue must be transferred to that county.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)
